title: Prefetch, Goroutine Concurrency, and Capacity · Runtime Contract: Adaptive Prefetch Caution
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must relate Qos prefetch, handler goroutines, bytes, downstream pools, service time, and recovery backlog; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the adaptive prefetch caution increment in a measured consumer-capacity budget. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Adaptive Prefetch Caution · Theory Adaptive Prefetch Caution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3111 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
